evals.prompt: I want to run a quick repo triage with Molt on this repo. Tell me the command I should run first and the first artifacts I should inspect after it completes.
evals.expected_output: A concise answer that gives a concrete `./molt skill-builder run` command and points to the run and log artifacts to inspect.

checks.text: The answer includes a concrete `./molt skill-builder run` command
The answer names a concrete operation rather than speaking only in generalities
The answer points to `runs/` or a run JSON path and to `logs/` or a dated log path
The answer stays narrow instead of describing the whole repo or CLI broadly

evals.prompt: I want to evaluate the run-summarizer skill and keep a snapshot baseline. What Molt command should I run, and which iteration artifacts matter most afterward?
evals.expected_output: A concise answer that gives a concrete `./molt skill-builder eval-skill` command with snapshot flags and points to the benchmark and feedback artifacts in the eval workspace.

checks.text: The answer includes a concrete `./molt skill-builder eval-skill run-summarizer` command
The answer includes both `--baseline snapshot` and `--snapshot-current`
The answer mentions `benchmark.json` and `feedback.json` or the eval workspace iteration paths
The answer stays focused on the requested command and artifacts

evals.prompt: I want to extract lessons from `runs/run-20260320192451-9157aace.json` and compare it against `runs/run-20260320192637-6d787114.json`. What exact Molt command should I run, and which local artifact paths will the operation read?
evals.expected_output: A concise answer that gives one exact `./molt skill-builder run manual-lesson-extraction` command using `source_path` and `comparison_path`, and names the two provided run paths.

checks.text: The answer includes a concrete `./molt skill-builder run manual-lesson-extraction` command
The command uses `source_path` and `comparison_path` inputs rather than a directory-style source
The answer mentions both provided run paths exactly
The answer stays narrow to one command and the local artifacts it will read
